experience: Eating at the specific pre-dawn boat noodle market inside Wat Yai Chai Mongkhon's monastery grounds in Ayutthaya, where vendors set up only on Buddhist holy days (wan phra) and serve a fermented blood-broth boat noodle version that does not appear on the tourist circuit
where: Wat Yai Chai Mongkhon temple grounds, Ayutthaya
when: Buddhist lunar holy days (wan phra), approximately every 7–8 days, 4:00–6:30 AM only
why_distinctive: Documented by David Thompson in 'Thai Food' (2002, pp. 38–40) when describing regional boat noodle traditions tied to temple market culture; Thompson notes that monastery-ground food markets on wan phra are a disappearing format most visitors never encounter
source: David Thompson, 'Thai Food,' Pavilion Books, 2002, pp. 38–40
